title: Veklom & ABIDE Sovereign Agentic Control Plane
tagline: ABIDE Proposes, CAPPO Disposes: The Sovereign Governance Force Field for Autonomous Agentic Workforces

highLevelGoals.title: Enforce Human Sovereignty via CAPPO Covenant Gate
highLevelGoals.description: Decouple AI planning from system authorization, ensuring that ABIDE only proposes execution graphs while CAPPO deterministically authorizes them.

highLevelGoals.title: Implement Tiered Hierarchical Evidence Anchoring
highLevelGoals.description: Resolve PGL ledger throughput bottlenecks by implementing a 4-tier Merkle-batched evidence architecture.

highLevelGoals.title: Deploy Live Capability Projection via Unified SDK
highLevelGoals.description: Transition from static SDK packages to live, signed capability snapshots that fail closed under network degradation.

highLevelGoals.title: Establish x402 Machine-to-Machine Settlement Rails
highLevelGoals.description: Integrate automated global payment settlements and smart contract decentralized liquidity execution into agentic workflows.

competitiveMoat.description: Translates unstructured human intent into a mathematically bounded, reviewable execution graph, preventing agentic drift and hallucination.
Every system state transition is irreversibly anchored to the original human intent via PGL Merkle-proofs.
Zero external managed database dependencies; strictly self-hosted PostgreSQL and local enclaves, eliminating Firebase/CDN leak vectors.

variables.name: Capability Drift Frequency
variables.impact: High - Triggers automatic graph re-verification and potential execution halt if drift exceeds 0.05%.

variables.name: Policy Compliance Probability
variables.impact: Critical - Deterministic CAPPO evaluation must equal 1.0 before any state-changing execution is scheduled.

variables.name: x402 Collateralization Ratio
variables.impact: Medium - Determines priority queue placement for multi-agent resource allocation.

academicGrounding.title: Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Events in a Distributed System
academicGrounding.author: Leslie Lamport
academicGrounding.summary: Foundational paper on logical clocks and event ordering, utilized to establish deterministic sequence numbers across distributed agentic actions.
academicGrounding.relevance: Ensures that the PGL ledger maintains a strict, unalterable causal sequence of agent actions.

academicGrounding.title: The Temporal Logic of Actions
academicGrounding.author: Leslie Lamport
academicGrounding.source: ACM Transactions on Programming Languages and Systems, 1994
academicGrounding.summary: Introduces TLA+, a formal specification language for describing and reasoning about concurrent and reactive systems.
academicGrounding.relevance: Used to model-check the state transitions of the ABIDE execution graph before compilation.

academicGrounding.title: Z3: An Efficient SMT Solver
academicGrounding.author: Leonardo de Moura, Nikolaj Bjørner
academicGrounding.source: Tools and Algorithms for the Construction and Analysis of Systems (TACAS), 2008
academicGrounding.summary: Seminal paper on the Z3 theorem prover, detailing SMT solving techniques for software verification.
academicGrounding.relevance: Powers the constraint evaluation engine that mathematically bounds agent execution parameters.

policies.name: CAPPO-01: No Unsigned Execution
policies.rule: Every state-changing action must carry a valid cryptographic signature from an authorized CAPPO gatekeeper.

policies.name: CAPPO-02: Sovereign Data Boundary
policies.rule: No tenant data or execution telemetry may be transmitted to external managed databases or CDNs.

capabilities.name: ABIDE Intent Compilation & Graph Synthesis
capabilities.purpose: Translates unstructured human intent into a deterministic, mathematically bounded execution graph.
capabilities.businessOutcome: Enables non-technical users to safely orchestrate complex, multi-agent workflows without manual coding.
capabilities.machineOutcome: Produces a validated JSON execution graph containing explicit dependencies, schemas, and resource limits.
preconditions: Capability graph snapshot is signed and within TTL
postconditions: Execution graph is registered in temporary memory
Graph is forwarded to CAPPO for policy evaluation

governance.delegations: Allowed to fallback to local CPU-based constraint solver if GPU cluster is degraded
governance.auditReqs: All compiled graphs must be logged to PGL
governance.killSwitchRules: Immediate halt if compiler generates cyclic dependencies

evidence.privateDetails: Contains raw user intent and compiled node parameters

verification.driftChecks: Daily verification of schema alignment with Interlink cAPI

jurisdictionConstraints: GDPR Article 22 compliance (no sole automated decision-making without human-in-the-loop)

capabilities.name: CAPPO Covenant Gate Policy Evaluation
capabilities.purpose: Deterministically evaluates proposed execution graphs against organizational rules and compliance boundaries.
capabilities.businessOutcome: Prevents unauthorized or high-risk agent actions before they execute, eliminating the risk of autonomous damage.
capabilities.machineOutcome: Returns a binary authorization decision (ALLOW/DENY) accompanied by a cryptographic signature.
preconditions: Execution graph is signed by ABIDE compiler
postconditions: Authorization decision is anchored to PGL
Execution is blocked if decision is DENY

governance.dataBoundaries: Strictly local execution; no external policy evaluation allowed
governance.delegations: None; policy evaluation cannot be delegated
governance.auditReqs: 100% of decisions must be written to PGL with zero-knowledge proof of inputs
governance.killSwitchRules: If policy engine is unreachable, all execution defaults to DENY (fail-closed)

evidence.privateDetails: Contains evaluated rules and matching graph nodes

verification.driftChecks: Continuous monitoring of policy file integrity hashes

jurisdictionConstraints: Compliance with local financial and data sovereignty laws

capabilities.name: Hierarchical Evidence Anchoring
capabilities.purpose: Cryptographically seals execution lineage, state transitions, and policy decisions using a tiered Merkle-batching architecture.
capabilities.businessOutcome: Provides mathematical proof of compliance and execution truth, eliminating audit disputes and enabling reusable blueprint economies.
capabilities.machineOutcome: Generates a verifiable Merkle proof anchored to the PGL ledger.
preconditions: Events are signed by the generating capability/agent
PGL node is synchronized and healthy
postconditions: Merkle root is written to the global ledger
Lineage is permanently queryable via transaction ID

exposureSurfaces.description: High-throughput gRPC endpoint for event anchoring

governance.budgetRules: Staked collateral on VNP determines write priority
governance.dataBoundaries: Ledger nodes must reside within designated sovereign jurisdictions
governance.delegations: Allowed to batch events locally during temporary ledger network partitions
governance.auditReqs: Ledger state must be audited weekly against external public blockchains
governance.killSwitchRules: Halt consensus if double-spend or state fork is detected

evidence.privateDetails: Contains transaction Merkle tree and state roots

verification.driftChecks: Continuous verification of block hashes against peer nodes

jurisdictionConstraints: Cryptographic proof of storage location required

capabilities.purpose: Discovers, normalizes, and routes capabilities using Model Context Protocol (MCP) supergraphs.
capabilities.businessOutcome: Eliminates hardcoded integrations and API hallucinations by providing agents with a live, verified tool registry.
capabilities.machineOutcome: Exposes a dynamic, strongly typed GraphQL and MCP interface representing the current system state.
postconditions: Tool execution is routed to the correct backend
Execution metrics are reported to VNP

exposureSurfaces.description: MCP endpoint for dynamic tool discovery

governance.dataBoundaries: Data payload must not cross designated jurisdictional boundaries during routing
governance.delegations: Allowed to route to local mock adapters if external provider is unreachable and simulation mode is active
governance.auditReqs: All routing decisions and latency metrics must be logged to VNP
governance.killSwitchRules: Halt routing to any capability that fails 3 consecutive health checks

evidence.privateDetails: Contains routing path and latency metrics

verification.driftChecks: Real-time schema validation against registered capability manifests

jurisdictionConstraints: Strict routing path enforcement based on data classification
jurisdictionPolicy.auditRetentionProfile: 30 Days Local, Merkle Root to PGL

files.content:
# Veklom & ABIDE Sovereign Agentic Control Plane

This repository contains the core architecture, schemas, and governance specifications for the Veklom and ABIDE agentic ecosystem. Built under the strict guidelines of the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) and locked under Constitution v4.02.1.

## Core Invariant: ABIDE Proposes, CAPPO Disposes

To prevent autonomous agentic drift, the planning layer (ABIDE) is mathematically decoupled from the authorization layer (CAPPO). ABIDE compiles human intent into a proposed execution graph, which CAPPO independently evaluates against deterministic policies before any state-changing action is executed.

## Getting Started

1. Deploy the sovereign BYOS backend using PostgreSQL.
2. Configure the Interlink cAPI router to discover local capabilities.
3. Initialize the CAPPO policy engine with your organizational rules.
4. Anchor all execution evidence to the PGL ledger.

# Capability Manifest

This manifest registers the core capabilities of the Veklom ecosystem under the global jurisdiction profile.

| Capability ID | Name | Canonical System | Maturity State | Verification State |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `cap-abide-intent-compiler` | ABIDE Intent Compiler | `abide-compiler-service` | Sovereign Production | Verified |
| `cap-cappo-policy-gate` | CAPPO Policy Gate | `cappo-policy-engine` | Sovereign Production | Verified |
| `cap-pgl-evidence-anchor` | Hierarchical Evidence Anchoring | `gnomledger-node` | Sovereign Production | Verified |
| `cap-interlink-mcp-router` | Dynamic Capability Router | `interlink-router` | Sovereign Production | Verified |

# Work Orders & Implementation Plan

This document outlines the active engineering work orders for the Veklom platform, structured as discrete agent packets.

## Active Packets

- **pkt-1**: Implement Hierarchical PGL Evidence Anchoring Engine
- **pkt-2**: Build MCP-Compliant Interlink cAPI Router with Signed Snapshots

agentPackets.title: Implement Hierarchical PGL Evidence Anchoring Engine
agentPackets.summary: Build the tiered evidence architecture (Tiers 1-4) to resolve PGL throughput bottlenecks and enable high-volume agentic event logging.
agentPackets.objective: Implement a Merkle-batched event stream that aggregates local agent actions and anchors the Merkle root to the PGL ledger synchronously only during critical state transitions.
agentPackets.scope: Modify the `gnomledger-node` repository to support local event buffering, Merkle tree generation, and batch anchoring endpoints.
agentPackets.contracts: The batch anchoring endpoint must accept a list of signed events, verify their signatures, construct a Merkle tree, and return the root hash along with a verifiable Merkle proof.
agentPackets.migrations: Database schema migration to support local event buffering tables in the PostgreSQL stub.
agentPackets.securityConstraints: All buffered events must be encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with keys managed inside the sovereign enclave.
definitionOfDone: Code passes all unit and integration tests
Merkle proof verification is mathematically proven in the test suite
No external Firebase or CDN dependencies are introduced
OpenTelemetry instrumentation is fully implemented and verified
agentPackets.rollbackNotes: If the batch anchoring engine fails, fall back to synchronous single-event anchoring while logging a degradation alert to VNP.

agentPackets.title: Build MCP-Compliant Interlink cAPI Router with Signed Snapshots
agentPackets.summary: Implement the live capability graph projection and signed snapshot mechanism for the Unified SDK to prevent mid-run graph mutation.
agentPackets.objective: Ensure that the Unified SDK can query a signed, immutable snapshot of the capability graph at the start of an execution run, failing closed if the snapshot expires or the router is unreachable.
agentPackets.scope: Modify the `interlink-cAPI` and `veklom-sdk` repositories to implement the signed snapshot protocol and fail-closed logic.
agentPackets.contracts: The SDK must reject any capability execution if the current time exceeds the snapshot TTL, or if the snapshot signature is invalid.
agentPackets.performanceTargets: Snapshot generation time < 5ms, SDK resolution overhead < 1ms.
agentPackets.securityConstraints: Snapshots must be signed using the platform's private Ed25519 key held inside the secure enclave.
definitionOfDone: SDK successfully fails closed when snapshot TTL expires
All MCP compliance tests pass successfully
Signature verification is implemented without external cryptographic dependencies
Code is fully documented and reviewed by the Lead Architect
agentPackets.rollbackNotes: In case of critical failure, revert the SDK to the last-known-good static capability mapping while raising a high-severity alert in the Approval Inbox.

E.reasoning: Efficiency compilation: latency 12.4ms, success rate 99%, with 4/4 capabilities specifying exact latency SLO goals.

R.reasoning: Academic validation check: 0/3 citations verified with complete resolvable identifiers, retrieval times, claim locations, and digital signatures.

C.reasoning: Compliance analysis: detected 22 declared tests across 4 capabilities. Drift prevention mechanisms active in 4/4 modules.

D.reasoning: Sovereignty check complete. Evaluated 4/4 capability jurisdiction profiles and 2 global policies. 0 profiles are missing.

S.reasoning: Settlement audit complete. Evaluated 4/4 capability pricing parameters. 4 capabilities are strictly machine-to-machine x402 settlement compliant.
